fitness_note: CHELSA v2.1 BIO12 (annual precip) — bias-corrected high-res; preferred over CHIRPS for static climatology.
MapSPAM ~10 km harvested-area fraction — coarse global default.
National EO cocoa map (~10 m, 2023) known to exist; upload to override MapSPAM for the SLE pilot.
CHELSA BIO1 = annual mean temperature; multi-band asset, band-resolved via this binding.
Cropping-rainfed yield gap — GAEZ v4 attainable-vs-actual or MapSPAM-derived; pilot binding to be uploaded.
Cropping-irrigated yield gap — GAEZ v4 irrigated attainable-vs-actual; pilot binding to be uploaded.
Mixed systems: composite of crop yield gap and livestock-productivity gap; livestock side often thinner — flag for upload.
Rangeland NPP gap (e.g. MODIS NPP vs CASA potential); livestock side often thinner — requires_upload by default.
Agro-pastoral composite (cropping yield-gap + NPP-gap) — pilot upload.
Tree-perennial production gap — MapSPAM-based attainable for the perennial; per-crop binding (cocoa/coffee/etc.) preferred.
CHELSA BIO5 = max temperature of warmest month — proxy for heat-stress exposure; multi-band asset, band-resolved.
WB PIP subnational poverty headcount at admin1 (admin2 where available).

Derived run-time layer. Inputs: glad_global_cropland_2019 + esa_worldcereal_2021 + glw4_livestock_2020 + fao_gmia_v5 + hansen_treecover2000 + mapspam_cocoa_2020. Recipe: methodology/farming_system_classifier.md. Default thresholds; tune per AOI.
Sierra Leone tuning: irrigation_share=0.10, livestock_mixed=3.0 TLU/km2, livestock_pastoral=15.0, tree_perennial_dominant=0.40. See farming_system_classifier.md §Per-AOI tuning.
BII (Newbold et al. 2016). Global 1 km. Use (1 - BII) as priority.
